content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer NNN.

You can do the following operations:


If NNN is even, choose to change NNN to either (3âŠ•N)+1(3 \oplus N) + 1(3âŠ•N)+1 or N2\dfrac{N}{2}2Nâ€‹.
If NNN is odd, change NNN to (3âŠ•N)+1(3 \oplus N) + 1(3âŠ•N)+1.
Here, âŠ•\oplusâŠ• denotes the Bitwise XOR operator.

Check if it is possible to go from NNN to 111. print Yes\text{Yes}Yes if it is possible, and No\text{No}No otherwise.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line Yes\text{Yes}Yes if you can go from NNN to 111, and No\text{No}No otherwise.


It is allowed to print each character in either case, i.e. YES\text{YES}YES, yes\text{yes}yes and yEs\text{yEs}yEs will all be accepted as positive responses.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : NNN is already 111.
Test Case 2 : N=2N = 2N=2 is even, and we choose to change it to N2\frac{N}{2}2Nâ€‹ to get 111 in one operation.
Test Case 3 : N=3N = 3N=3 is odd, and we are forced to change NNN to (3âŠ•N+1)=1(3 \oplus N + 1) = 1(3âŠ•N+1)=1.
